title: linaria caesia subsp. decumbens
intro: Linaria caesia subsp. decumbens, also known as the decumbent toadflax, is a subspecies of the toadflax plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Linaria caesia subsp. decumbens include its ability to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic properties. Linaria caesia subsp. decumbens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
